headlines.title: Brazil and Italy rule out Ebola in previously suspected cases
headlines.description: The World Health Organization’s top doctor traveled to the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo over the weekend, as suspected cases and deaths continue to mount.

headlines.title: Around 1 in 5 young people use AI chatbots for mental health advice, survey finds
headlines.description: Nearly 1 in 5 adolescents and young adults are turning to AI chatbots for advice when they’re sad, angry, nervous or stressed, according to a new study.

headlines.title: GLP-1 overdoses increase at an alarming rate as weight loss drugs become easier to buy online
headlines.description: Within 24 hours of injecting the first dose of a weight loss medication she received following a visit with a telehealth doctor, Karleigh McClain was admitted to the hospital, she said

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: At the Enhanced Games, drugs don’t get athletes banned. They could get them rich.
headlines.description: When Shania Collins was first approached about taking performance-enhancing drugs last year, it made her nervous enough to contact two members of the Drug Enforcement Administration — her parents.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: Kick Off Summer 2026 Strong With Start TODAY’s Strength Plan
headlines.description: TODAY fitness contributor Stephanie Mansour and nutrition and health expert Joy Bauer stop by Studio 1A to break down June’s Start TODAY 30-day strength plan to kick off your summer strong and confident. They show effective strength training moves and explain how the plan can improve brain health and boost your mood.

headlines.title: Get an Inside Look at the 2026 'Big Slick' Charity Weekend
headlines.description: The Big Slick is an annual fundraiser hosted by celebrities Paul Rudd, Jason Sudeikis, Heidi Gardner, Rob Riggle, and more, who head home to Kansas City for a weekend jam-packed with entertainment and charity. The weekend events raise money for pediatric cancer research and support for Kansas City’s Children’s Mercy Hospital. Since starting in 2010 as a poker tournament, the Big Slick has raised more than $34 million and continues to grow. TODAY’s Al Roker reports.

headlines.title: New Pancreatic Cancer Drug Doubles Survival Rate in Trials
headlines.description: A new drug, decades in the making, is showing game-changing results for those battling pancreatic cancer. NBC’s medical contributor Dr. Natalie Azar joins TODAY with what you need to know about the breakthrough medication that doubled survival time and slowed tumor progression in patients during trials and how it could potentially help treat other cancers.

headlines.title: Hospitals in Brazil Test Two Patients With Ebola-Like Symptoms
headlines.description: The director general of the World Health Organization has arrived in the Democratic Republic of Congo as doctors on the ground race to play catch up against the third largest Ebola outbreak in history. In Rio De Janeiro and São Paulo, Brazil, hospitals are testing two patients with Ebola-like symptoms. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.
